); }; export default AddressModal; ``` ### How to Use: 1. **Import the Component**: You can import this component into any file where you need an address input modal. 2. **Toggle Modal**: Clicking the "Open Address Modal" button will open the modal, and clicking outside or hitting the cancel button will close it. 3. **Form Submission**: The form inside the modal allows you to enter street, ZIP code, and city. You can handle form submission as needed. This implementation uses Tailwind CSS for styling and the Shadcn UI Button component for a consistent look-and-feel.

AUTOMATISCH GEFUNDENE REFERENZ-DOKUMENTATION: --- REFERENZ: Components > Tables --- #### Tables The table component represents a set of structured elements made up of rows and columns as table cells that can be used to show data sets to your website users. Get started with multiple variants and styles of these table components built with the utility classes from Tailwind CSS and components from Flowbite. ##### Default table Use the following example of a responsive table component to show multiple rows and columns of text data. {{< example id="default-table-example" github="components/tables.md" class="overflow-hidden" show_dark=true >}}
Product name Color Category Price
Apple MacBook Pro 17" Silver Laptop $2999
Microsoft Surface Pro White Laptop PC $1999
Magic Mouse 2 Black Accessories $99
{{< /example >}} ##### Highlight Accentuate certain elements inside the table such as the rows, columns or data cells based on your needs. ###### Striped rows Use this example to increase the readability of the data sets by alternating the background color of every second table row. {{< example id="table-striped-rows-example" github="components/tables.md" class="overflow-hidden" show_dark=true >}}
Product name Color Category Price Action
Apple MacBook Pro 17" Silver Laptop $2999 Edit
Microsoft Surface Pro White Laptop PC $1999 Edit
Magic Mouse 2 Black Accessories $99 Edit
Google Pixel Phone Gray Phone $799 Edit
Apple Watch 5 Red Wearables $999 Edit
{{< /example >}} ###### Striped columns [... gekürzt ...] BENUTZERANFRAGE: Kannst Du mir mit shadcn ein formular mit name, adresse und geburtsdatum erstellen? Gerne mit date-picker.

Gerne! Hier ist ein Beispiel für ein Formular mit Shadcn UI, das Name, Adresse und Geburtsdatum enthält, einschließlich eines Date-Pickers: ```jsx import React from 'react'; import { Button, Form, FormControl, FormDescription, FormField, FormItem, FormLabel, Input, DatePicker } from '@shadcn/ui'; const AddressForm = () => { return (

Address Information

e.preventDefault()}> ( Name This is the user's full name. )} /> ( Address Please enter your full address. )} /> ( Date of Birth field.onChange(date)} /> Enter your date of birth. )} />
); }; export default AddressForm; ``` ### How to Use: 1. **Install Shadcn UI**: Make sure you have Shadcn UI installed in your project. ```bash npm install @shadcn/ui ``` 2. **Import the Component**: Import this component into any file where you need an address form. 3. **Form Submission**: The form inside the component allows you to enter a name, address, and date of birth. You can handle form submission as needed. ### Notes: - `control` should be defined in your parent component or using `useForm` hook from React Hook Form if you are managing form state. - Replace the placeholder values with actual data handling logic according to your application's requirements. This example uses Shadcn UI components for a consistent look-and-feel, and Tailwind CSS for styling.
